The chat() helper uses requests.post() to send a JSON payload to f"{OLLAMA_URL}/api/chat". The payload needs three keys: "model" (set to MODEL), "messages" (a list of dicts with "role" and "content"), and "stream" (set to False). The response content is at response.json()["message"]["content"]. Wrap everything in try/except requests.RequestException and raise HTTPException(status_code=503) in the except block.

Each Pydantic model inherits from BaseModel. SummarizeRequest needs one field: text: str. ClassifyRequest needs text: str and categories: list[str]. GenerateRequest needs prompt: str and max_tokens: int = 200 (the default value makes it optional). AIResponse needs result: str. That's it -- Pydantic handles validation automatically.

All three endpoints follow the same pattern: (1) build a system_prompt string with task-specific instructions, (2) call result = chat(system_prompt, req.text) passing the user's input, (3) return AIResponse(result=result). For /classify, join the categories into a string with ', '.join(req.categories) and include them in the system prompt. For /generate, use req.prompt instead of req.text.
